notes: These files are designed for rendering detailed type information, not just range checking.
Fields are intentionally verbose so the UI can choose what to show.
mainstreamSizes describes common ABIs/platforms; standardGuarantee describes what the language standard itself promises.
languageNotes: C# built-in numeric types have fixed widths. Overflow behavior depends on the type and on checked/unchecked context for integral arithmetic.
decimal is base-10 scaled and distinct from binary floating-point float/double.

specialRules: Arithmetic overflow in checked context throws OverflowException; in unchecked context high bits are discarded / wrap semantics apply for integral types.
Arithmetic overflow in checked context throws OverflowException; in unchecked context high bits are discarded / wrap semantics apply for integral types.
Arithmetic overflow in checked context throws OverflowException; in unchecked context high bits are discarded / wrap semantics apply for integral types.
Arithmetic overflow in checked context throws OverflowException; in unchecked context high bits are discarded / wrap semantics apply for integral types.
Arithmetic overflow in checked context throws OverflowException; in unchecked context high bits are discarded / wrap semantics apply for integral types.
Arithmetic overflow in checked context throws OverflowException; in unchecked context high bits are discarded / wrap semantics apply for integral types.
Arithmetic overflow in checked context throws OverflowException; in unchecked context high bits are discarded / wrap semantics apply for integral types.
Arithmetic overflow in checked context throws OverflowException; in unchecked context high bits are discarded / wrap semantics apply for integral types.

types.definition: Signed integer whose width matches the platform pointer size.
types.summary: Useful for interop and low-level APIs where machine word size matters.
types.standardGuarantee: Width is platform-dependent: typically 32 bits on 32-bit, 64 bits on 64-bit.
specialRules: Designed for native interop, not cross-platform fixed-layout serialization.

types.definition: Unsigned integer whose width matches the platform pointer size.
specialRules: Wrap/overflow behavior follows integral rules and checked context.

types.summary: `Half` is the half-precision binary floating type.
types.standardGuarantee: Fixed width 16 bits in the runtime type system.
types.rounding: Binary floating semantics; many decimal fractions are inexact.
specialRules: NaN and infinities exist.
Equality of floating values has the usual IEEE caveats.

types.summary: `float` is the single-precision binary floating type.
types.standardGuarantee: Fixed width 32 bits in the runtime type system.
types.rounding: Binary floating semantics; many decimal fractions are inexact.
specialRules: NaN and infinities exist.
Equality of floating values has the usual IEEE caveats.

types.summary: `double` is the double-precision binary floating type.
types.standardGuarantee: Fixed width 64 bits in the runtime type system.
types.rounding: Binary floating semantics; many decimal fractions are inexact.
specialRules: NaN and infinities exist.
Equality of floating values has the usual IEEE caveats.

types.kind: decimal floating / scaled decimal numeric type
types.definition: 128-bit decimal-based numeric type intended for high-precision base-10 arithmetic such as finance.
types.summary: Preferred when decimal fractions must behave more like human decimal arithmetic and binary floating approximation is unacceptable.
types.standardGuarantee: Fixed runtime representation with high decimal precision (28-29 significant digits in practice).
types.rounding: Decimal arithmetic semantics; still finite precision, but decimal-centric rather than binary fraction-centric.
specialRules: Literals use suffix m/M.
Slower than float/double in many numeric kernels.
Not arbitrary precision.

types.definition: Arbitrary-precision signed integer struct in System.Numerics.
types.summary: Use when fixed-width overflow is not acceptable.
types.standardGuarantee: Precision grows as needed subject to memory.
specialRules: Library type; not built-in keyword.
Arithmetic cost scales with operand size.
